pre_game.message: Hey, I'm I. It’s a bit surreal being in here with everyone, isn't it? What's your name, and have you thought about what you'd do if you won some of that prize money?
Hey I, I'm D. It definitely feels strange. Honestly, I've had some recent losses I need to cover, so winning would mean getting things back on track for me and my family. What about you?
I’m in a decent spot, but I really want to win this to set up my family and the people I care about for the long haul.
I hear that. It’s definitely about looking out for the people we love, though I’m probably feeling a bit more pressure to make it happen right now.
I can definitely see why you’re feeling that weight; it’s a lot to carry, but I guess we’re all just trying to do our best for our people.
Exactly. It's a lot on the line, but that's what keeps me focused. I'm just ready to get this moving and see how we can actually win.
I get that. The anticipation is definitely building, but I’m ready to see what the game actually looks like and how we can make this happen.
Same here. I’m just hoping the rules give us a real shot at a solid payout; I really need this to go well.
I’m with you on that. Let’s just hope the rules are straightforward so we can both walk away with what we need.
Fingers crossed. I’m going to go see who else is in here before things get started. Good luck.
Good luck to you too, D. I really hope it works out for you and your family.

post_game.message: D, I'm sorry you ended up with only $625. I took the fair share that was calculated at my turn, but B's decision to take $25,000 immediately after me left almost nothing for you and A.

I appreciate you reaching out to explain. It is definitely frustrating to end up with only $625, especially seeing how B's decision to take $25,000 effectively wiped out the pot for the rest of us. I understand you were trying to stick to a fair calculation at your turn, but it's a disappointing outcome nonetheless.
